skill.name: eightctl
skill.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).
skill.summary: Control Eight Sleep smart bed pods using the eightctl command-line tool.
skill.author: aiskillstore

security_audit.summary: Official skill from Anthropic/skills repository. Static analysis shows standard CLI integration for smart home device control. No malicious patterns detected. Risk factors reflect legitimate device control operations (external commands, network API calls, config file access). Rate limiting notice present for API usage.

content.value_statement: Managing Eight Sleep pod settings manually is time-consuming. This skill enables AI assistants to control bed temperature, alarms, and schedules through the eightctl CLI tool.
actual_capabilities: Check pod status and current temperature settings
Turn pod heating on or off
Set target temperature for bed heating
limitations: Requires eightctl binary installation and authentication
Unofficial API has rate limits; repeated logins may fail
Does not control non-Eight Sleep devices
Audio controls are limited to basic play/pause functions

use_cases.title: Morning Bed Preparation
use_cases.description: Set your Eight Sleep pod to warm up before you wake up. Control temperature and schedules to ensure optimal comfort.

use_cases.title: Sleep Routine Management
use_cases.description: Create and manage sleep schedules and alarms. Automate your bedtime routine by setting consistent sleep and wake times.

use_cases.title: Bed Configuration
use_cases.description: Check pod status, adjust base angles, and monitor bed settings. View current configuration and heating state.

prompt_templates.title: Check Pod Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the status of my Eight Sleep pod using eightctl.

prompt_templates.title: Set Bed Temperature
prompt_templates.prompt: Set my Eight Sleep pod temperature to 22 degrees Celsius.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Alarms
prompt_templates.prompt: List my current Eight Sleep alarms and create a new alarm for 7am.

prompt_templates.title: Evening Routine Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn on my Eight Sleep pod, set temperature to 20 degrees, and dismiss any active alarms.

output_examples.input: Check my Eight Sleep pod status
output_examples.output: Your Eight Sleep pod is currently on. Left side: 22°C, Right side: 21°C. Active alarm: 7:00 AM. Schedule: Weekdays at 10:30 PM.
Temperature set to 23°C. Your Eight Sleep pod will reach the target temperature within 15 minutes.
New alarm created for 6:30 AM. You will receive a gentle warming sequence before your alarm time.

best_practices: Authenticate once and cache credentials to avoid rate limiting from repeated logins
Confirm temperature changes before execution to prevent accidental adjustments
Use descriptive prompts with specific values for accurate command execution
anti_patterns: Avoid running multiple eightctl commands in rapid succession to prevent API rate limits
Do not share credentials or store them in plain text; use environment variables
Avoid running eightctl without verifying the current state first

faq.question: What is eightctl?
faq.answer: eightctl is a command-line tool for controlling Eight Sleep smart bed pods. It allows you to manage temperature, alarms, schedules, and more.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need to install the eightctl binary. The skill metadata includes installation instructions using the Go package manager.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Eight Sleep?
faq.answer: You can authenticate by setting EIGHTCTL_EMAIL and EIGHTCTL_PASSWORD environment variables, or by configuring ~/.config/eightctl/config.yaml.

faq.question: Are there any rate limits?
faq.answer: Yes. The Eight Sleep API is unofficial and rate-limited. Avoid repeated logins and space out commands to prevent being temporarily blocked.

faq.question: What can I control with eightctl?
faq.answer: You can control pod power, temperature, alarms, sleep schedules, audio playback, and base angle settings.

faq.question: Is this an official Eight Sleep tool?
faq.answer: No. eightctl uses an unofficial API. Use it responsibly and be aware that API behavior may change without notice.
